prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: atom‐resolved Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), and the dual descriptor f⁰ for formaldehyde (CH2O)
Level of theory: DFT, tier “standard” → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF  (invoked through chemkit)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied formaldehyde.xyz (no additional optimisation; single-point Fukui analysis at this geometry → DFT//input)
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet); cation and anion states were run as doublets (charge ±1, mult 2) per default protocol
Convergence & caveats: SCF for the N, N–1, and N+1 states converged; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true.  Engine checks returned three informational warnings (reproduced verbatim below).  Sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f⁻) = 1.0000, so charge conservation tests passed.  No other warnings or fall-backs reported.
Live log: the run streamed to a live .out file in the working directory while executing (path was announced at launch by chemkit; retain for full SCF details and reproducing).

Selected numerical results (units: e ≡ elementary charge)

3           H       –0.08826015 0.21005551 0.06089768  –0.29831566

Reactive sites
• Most electrophilic site (largest f⁺):  C (atom 2, index 1)  → 0.70839130 e
• Most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻):  O (atom 1, index 0)  → 0.46742464 e
